title: Fantoma Euro-Asiei bântuie Rusia și asta explică marea strategie a Kremlinului
summary: The article traces the deep ideological roots of Eurasianism that underpin Russia's grand strategy and its war against Ukraine. It argues that the Kremlin's ambitions go far beyond kleptocracy, driven instead by a centuries-old geopolitical vision of Russia as a unique Eurasian civilization opposed to the West. The piece surveys key thinkers from Dostoevsky through pan-Slavists to neo-Eurasianists like Dugin, showing how this ideology shapes Putin's goal of reconstituting a vast Eurasian space.
talking_points: Russia's war on Ukraine is driven not merely by kleptocratic greed but by a deep-rooted Eurasian geopolitical ideology seeking dominance over the former Soviet space
Eurasianism has centuries-old intellectual roots in Russian identity debates about whether Russia is European or Asian, from pan-Slavists like Danilevsky to emigré theorists like Trubetskoy
Trump's failure to broker peace stems partly from amateurism and corruption but also from a realpolitik framework that cannot grasp Russia's civilizational worldview
Key thinkers like Lev Gumilev bridged interwar Eurasianism with post-Soviet neo-Eurasianism, perpetuating the idea of Russia as a unique continental civilization
The Kremlin views itself as leading a Eurasia-first opposition to the West, not as a neo-communist project but as a civilizational mission
author: Desk Russie
